title: South Korea Launches Massive $1 Trillion Tech Investment Plan
subtitle: The government and major companies aim to lead the global AI and robotics market by 2028.

summary.en: South Korea has announced a massive $1 trillion investment plan to boost its artificial intelligence and semiconductor industries. The project aims to build new chip factories, data centers, and robotics technology over the next few years. President Lee Jae-myung stated that these technologies are essential for the country's future growth. While the plan promises economic benefits, it also faces challenges from labor unions and debates over wealth distribution. The initiative is part of a broader effort to stay competitive against global rivals like China and Japan.
summary.zh: 南韓宣布了一項高達一兆美元的龐大投資計畫，旨在提振其人工智慧與半導體產業。該專案目標在未來幾年內建立新的晶片工廠、資料中心與機器人技術。總統李在明表示，這些技術對於該國的未來成長至關重要。雖然該計畫承諾帶來經濟效益，但也面臨來自工會的挑戰以及關於財富分配的爭論。這項倡議是南韓為了在與中國及日本等全球競爭對手的較量中保持競爭力，所採取的一系列廣泛努力之一。

paragraphs.en: South Korea has announced a major new plan to invest $1 trillion into its technology sector. This massive project aims to expand the country's production of semiconductors—the tiny chips that power computers and smartphones—and advance its artificial intelligence (AI) capabilities. The government, working alongside top tech companies like Samsung and SK Hynix, hopes to complete these goals by 2028. This move comes as the global demand for AI technology continues to grow rapidly.
paragraphs.zh: 南韓宣布了一項總額達一兆美元的重大新計畫，旨在投資其科技產業。這項龐大的計畫目標在於擴大該國半導體——即驅動電腦與智慧型手機的微小晶片——的生產量，並提升其人工智慧（AI）實力。政府正與三星（Samsung）及 SK 海力士（SK Hynix）等頂尖科技公司合作，期望在二〇二八年之前達成這些目標。此舉正值全球對人工智慧技術的需求持續快速成長之際。

paragraphs.en: According to BBC News, the plan is part of the country’s "Three Mega Projects." These projects focus on building new chip manufacturing hubs, data centers, and robotics technology. President Lee Jae-myung announced the plan in a televised speech, explaining that the country must move quickly to stay ahead of international competitors. "We must secure the core elements of AI faster than any other country," Lee said. He described semiconductors, physical AI, and data centers as the three main parts of a "great leap forward" for the nation.
paragraphs.zh: 根據 BBC 新聞報導，該計畫是南韓「三大巨型專案」的一部分。這些專案聚焦於建立新的晶片製造中心、資料中心以及機器人技術。總統李在明（Lee Jae-myung）在電視演說中宣布了這項計畫，並解釋該國必須迅速採取行動，以保持在國際競爭對手中的領先地位。李在明表示：「我們必須比任何國家都更快掌握人工智慧的核心要素。」他將半導體、實體人工智慧與資料中心描述為國家「大躍進」的三大支柱。

paragraphs.en: One of the most important goals of this plan is to help areas outside of the capital, Seoul. Currently, most of South Korea's advanced factories are located in or near the capital city. President Lee noted that moving these industries to other regions is a matter of "survival." He argued that spreading out the factories will help fix the long-standing problem of rural decline and create a more balanced economy. By building new infrastructure in the southwest provinces, the government hopes to create jobs and support communities that have been left behind by the recent tech boom.
paragraphs.zh: 這項計畫最重要的目標之一，是協助首都首爾以外的地區發展。目前，南韓大多數先進工廠都位於首都或其周邊地區。李在明指出，將這些產業轉移至其他區域是攸關「生存」的問題。他主張，分散工廠位置將有助於解決長期存在的城鄉發展不均問題，並創造更平衡的經濟。透過在西南省份建設新基礎設施，政府希望創造就業機會，並支持那些在近期科技繁榮中被遺忘的社區。

paragraphs.en: Ars Technica reports that a large portion of the funding, about $585 billion, will be used by Samsung and SK Hynix to build new chip fabrication plants. The government’s main goal is to double the country's production of dynamic random-access memory (DRAM) within the next five years. DRAM is a type of memory chip used in almost all modern electronic devices. As AI technology becomes more common, the world has faced a shortage of these chips, which has caused prices for consumer electronics to rise. By increasing production, South Korea hopes to ease this global supply strain.
paragraphs.zh: 科技媒體 Ars Technica 報導指出，這筆資金中的一大部分，約五千八百五十億美元，將由三星與 SK 海力士用於興建新的晶片製造廠。政府的主要目標是在未來五年內，將該國動態隨機存取記憶體（DRAM）的產量翻倍。動態隨機存取記憶體是幾乎所有現代電子裝置都會使用的一種記憶體晶片。隨著人工智慧技術日益普及，全球面臨這些晶片的短缺，導致消費性電子產品價格上漲。透過提高產量，南韓希望緩解全球供應緊張的局面。

paragraphs.en: Beyond chips, the plan includes a major focus on robotics. Hyundai Motor Company is currently working to mass-produce humanoid robots—robots that look and move like humans—developed by its subsidiary, Boston Dynamics. These machines are designed to take over difficult or dangerous tasks in automotive factories and other workplaces. However, this part of the plan has met some resistance. Ars Technica noted that labor unions in South Korea are pushing back against the idea of robots entering the workforce, fearing that they might replace human workers.
paragraphs.zh: 除了晶片之外，該計畫也將重點放在機器人技術上。現代汽車（Hyundai Motor Company）目前正致力於量產由其子公司波士頓動力（Boston Dynamics）所開發的人形機器人，這些機器人的外觀與動作都與人類相似。這些機器旨在接手汽車工廠及其他工作場所中困難或危險的任務。然而，計畫的這一部分遭遇了一些阻力。Ars Technica 指出，南韓的工會正反對機器人進入職場，擔憂它們可能會取代人類勞工。

paragraphs.en: There are also ongoing public debates regarding the massive profits that major chipmakers have earned during the recent AI boom. Some policymakers have proposed ways to distribute this wealth more fairly across the country. Additionally, while the investment is seen as a way to ensure future growth, some investors have expressed concern about the huge amounts of money being spent. This uncertainty has caused some stock prices to fluctuate in recent days.
paragraphs.zh: 關於大型晶片製造商在近期人工智慧熱潮中所賺取的巨額利潤，公眾也存在持續的辯論。一些政策制定者已提出方案，希望更公平地在全國分配這些財富。此外，儘管這項投資被視為確保未來成長的途徑，但部分投資人對投入的鉅額資金表示擔憂。這種不確定性導致近期部分股票價格出現波動。

paragraphs.en: Despite these challenges, the South Korean government remains committed to the plan. The country is under pressure to keep up with global rivals like Taiwan, China, and Japan, which are also investing heavily in their own tech industries. With US tech giants like Google, Amazon, and Meta planning to spend hundreds of billions of dollars on AI infrastructure this year, South Korea sees this investment as a vital step to maintain its position as a global leader in technology. As the world becomes more dependent on AI, the success of these projects will likely play a major role in the country's economic future.
paragraphs.zh: 儘管面臨這些挑戰，南韓政府仍致力於推動該計畫。該國正面臨必須跟上台灣、中國與日本等全球競爭對手的壓力，這些國家也在各自的科技產業投入大量資金。隨著 Google、亞馬遜（Amazon）與 Meta 等美國科技巨頭計畫在今年投入數千億美元於人工智慧基礎設施，南韓將此投資視為維持其全球科技領先地位的關鍵一步。隨著世界對人工智慧的依賴加深，這些計畫的成敗很可能對該國的經濟未來扮演重要角色。

keyTerms.definitionEn: To assume control of a task or responsibility previously done by someone or something else.
keyTerms.definitionZh: 接管、取代（某人的工作或任務）。
keyTerms.noteZh: 常見於「接管公司」之意，此處指機器人取代人類執行危險任務。文中：These machines are designed to take over difficult or dangerous tasks in automotive factories and other workplaces.

keyTerms.definitionEn: To express strong disagreement or resistance to a plan or idea.
keyTerms.noteZh: 字面上有「推回去」的意思，這裡引申為對政策或計畫表達反對意見。文中：Ars Technica noted that labor unions in South Korea are pushing back against the idea of robots entering the workforce, fearing that they might replace human workers.

keyTerms.definitionEn: To fail to make progress at the same speed as others; to be neglected.
keyTerms.definitionZh: 被遺忘、被拋在後頭、未享受到發展紅利。
keyTerms.noteZh: 常指物理上的「遺落」，這裡指經濟發展中被忽略的地區。文中：By building new infrastructure in the southwest provinces, the government hopes to create jobs and support communities that have been left behind by the recent tech boom.

sources.name: BBC Business
sources.title: South Korea unveils $880bn chip and AI investment plan

sources.name: Ars Technica
sources.title: South Korea to spend $1T on more memory chip production and humanoid robots

previousArticles.title: South Korea Launches Massive Investment in AI and Chip Technology
previousArticles.summaryEn: South Korea has announced a massive investment plan worth hundreds of billions of dollars to advance its AI and semiconductor industries. The project focuses on building new factories, data centers, and robotics technology to keep up with global demand. President Lee Jae-myung described the initiative as a matter of national survival. While companies like Samsung and SK Hynix expect to benefit, the plan also aims to help rural areas outside of Seoul. However, the move has sparked debates about wealth distribution and the future of human workers in the face of new robotics.

quiz.question: What is the primary reason the South Korean government wants to shift factory locations away from the capital?

options.A: To reduce the high costs of land and labor in Seoul.
options.B: To address the issue of rural decline and create a more balanced national economy.
options.C: To protect the factories from potential natural disasters near the capital.
options.D: To allow Samsung and SK Hynix to work closer to international shipping ports.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 B。根據文章第三段，總統 Lee 指出將工廠遷出首爾是為了「生存」（a matter of survival），目的是解決長期存在的鄉村衰退問題，並創造更平衡的經濟發展。選項 A 文中未提及成本問題；選項 C 與地理安全無關；選項 D 雖然提到工廠遷移，但並未提及港口因素，故皆為錯誤選項。

quiz.question: Based on the article, why might the South Korean government face difficulty in fully implementing its robotics plan?

options.A: The technology for humanoid robots is currently too expensive to mass-produce.
options.B: International competitors like Taiwan and China have already banned the use of such robots.
options.C: There is significant opposition from labor unions concerned about job security.
options.D: Boston Dynamics lacks the necessary expertise to develop robots for automotive factories.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 C。根據文章第五段，雖然政府計畫推廣機器人，但勞工工會（labor unions）對此表示反對，擔心機器人進入職場會取代人類工作。選項 A 文中提到的是 mass-produce，並未說太貴；選項 B 文中未提及國際禁令；選項 D 與事實相反，文中提到 Hyundai 正在與 Boston Dynamics 合作開發，並未質疑其專業度。

quiz.question: In the fourth paragraph, what does the word 'strain' mean in the phrase 'ease this global supply strain'?

options.A: A state of tension or pressure caused by high demand.
options.B: A physical injury caused by overworking.
options.C: A specific variety of a biological organism.
options.D: A feeling of deep sadness or emotional burden.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 A。在此語境中，supply strain 指的是全球對晶片需求過大，導致供應鏈出現壓力或緊繃狀態。選項 B 是指肌肉拉傷，不符合語境；選項 C 是指生物學上的「菌株」或「變種」，與晶片供應無關；選項 D 是指情緒上的壓力，雖然字面意思接近，但此處討論的是經濟供應鏈，而非個人情緒，故 A 為最佳解釋。

quiz.question: What is the central focus of the South Korean government's new $1 trillion investment plan?

options.A: To prioritize the development of humanoid robots over other technological advancements.
options.B: To increase the stock prices of Samsung and SK Hynix to help investors.
options.C: To address the environmental impact of current semiconductor manufacturing factories.
options.D: To strengthen the nation's tech sector and maintain global competitiveness in AI and chip production.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 D。文章開頭與結尾皆強調，韓國政府投入 1 兆美元是為了擴大半導體生產、提升 AI 能力，並在與台灣、中國、日本及美國科技巨頭的全球競爭中保持領先地位。選項 A 只是計畫的一部分，非核心目標；選項 B 是投資者的擔憂，非計畫目的；選項 C 文中完全未提及環境保護議題，故 D 為最完整的論點總結。
